ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-21-2024 Continued To Jun-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-18-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

David s. Pawlowski, Esq., via Zoom, for Petitioner.

At the court's query, counsel offers argument regarding whether the presumption in Probate Code 6124 should not apply in this matter. Counsel reports that the original will was likely taken when Decedent's home was burglarized. At conclusion of argument, the court advises that it is satisfied that the previously referenced Probate Code does not apply.

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed. The lost will dated Nov-03-1993 is admitted to probate. David Dea is appointed Administrator with will annexed, with full authority. Bond is set at $850,000. Amy Harrington is appointed Probate Referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Dec-17-2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cabrera. Not Reported.
